title: Operations & Security - Best Practices
headings: Think of It Like Managing a Building
body: Operations & Security Learn how to keep your OPCP environment running smoothly and securely — explained through the analogy of managing a building. Think of It Like Managing a Building Imagine you own a large office building. You need to keep the lights on, the elevators running, and the doors locked. Managing your OPCP environment works the same way: Resource management is like monitoring electricity and water usage — you track what is being consumed and plan for peak demand. Maintenance is lik

title: Performance & Troubleshooting - Best Practices
body: Performance & Troubleshooting Understand how to keep your OPCP services running at peak efficiency and what to do when something goes wrong — no technical expertise required. Performance Optimization Think of your cloud environment like a car engine. A well-tuned engine runs smoothly, uses fuel efficiently, and lasts longer. Here is how to keep your OPCP "engine" in top shape. Right-Sizing Resources Imagine buying a delivery truck to carry a single envelope — that is wasted capacity. Conversely,

title: Network Architecture - Core Concepts - OPCP Training
headings: Bandwidth — The Width of the Road
The City Analogy: Number of Lanes
body: Network Architecture Intermediate Understanding Network Architecture In OPCP, nodes don't operate in isolation — they need to communicate with each other and with the outside world. Network architecture defines how these connections are organized, secured, and optimized. Think of it like a city's road system . There are main highways (public network) that connect the city to the rest of the world, and internal streets (private network) that link buildings to each other. Some roads are wider and

title: Node Lifecycle - Core Concepts - OPCP Training
headings: The Office Analogy: Signing the Lease
The Office Analogy: Setting Up Furniture
body: Node Lifecycle Intermediate Understanding the Node Lifecycle Every node in OPCP goes through a series of stages during its existence — from the moment you request it to the moment it is retired. Understanding these stages helps you plan projects, set expectations with customers, and anticipate what happens at each step. Think of it like renting and furnishing an office space . You don't just walk in and start working — there's a process of signing the lease, setting up furniture, using the space

title: Node Types - Core Concepts - OPCP Training
body: Node Types Intermediate Choosing the Right Node Not every job requires the same tool. You wouldn't use a delivery truck to commute to work, and you wouldn't try to move furniture in a compact car. The same principle applies to nodes in OPCP — different workloads need different types of computing power. Think of node types like choosing a vehicle . Each type is designed for specific tasks, and picking the right one means better performance and better value for your customer. Node Types Comparison

title: Resource Allocation - Core Concepts - OPCP Training
headings: The Budget Analogy: Number of Employees
The Budget Analogy: Filing Cabinet Size
body: Resource Allocation Intermediate What is Resource Allocation? When you set up a node in OPCP, you need to decide how much computing power to assign to it. This is called resource allocation — the process of dividing available resources among your workloads. Think of it like dividing a department budget . You have a total amount to work with, and you need to distribute it wisely across salaries, equipment, travel, and supplies. Give too much to one area and others suffer. Give too little everywhe

title: What is OPCP? - Introduction - OPCP Skillhub

You have three options: A shared hostel — cheap, but you share everything with strangers. Noisy, unpredictable, and you have no control over who comes and goes. Building your own house — total control, but incredibly expensive. You handle plumbing, electricity, security, and maintenance all by yourself. body: Backup and Recovery Intermediate 🇫🇷 Français Why Back Up Your Data? Imagine all the files in your office disappearing overnight — client contracts, financial reports, ongoing projects. A backup is your safety net: a copy of your data stored separately, ready to be restored when something goes wrong. body: Sauvegarde et Récupération Intermédiaire 🇬🇧 English Pourquoi sauvegarder vos données ? Imaginez que tous les dossiers de votre bureau disparaissent du jour au lendemain — contrats clients, rapports financiers, projets en cours. La sauvegarde est votre filet de sécurité : une copie de vos données stockée séparément, prête à être restaurée en cas de problème. headings: Ressources Réservées vs. À la Demande
Ce qu'il Faut Dire aux Clients
body: Configuration des Nœuds Intermédiaire Présentation La configuration des nœuds détermine le comportement et les performances de vos ressources cloud. Chaque option de configuration affecte directement l'expérience de vos clients — de la rapidité de réponse de leurs applications au maintien en ligne de leur service pendant les opérations de maintenance. Analogie : Configurer un nœud est comme aménager un nouveau magasin. Vous décidez des horaires d'ouverture (disponibilité), du nombre de caisses à
